overview: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is a flagship public health assurance scheme of the Government of India, implemented by the National Health Authority (NHA), providing health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to approximately 12 crore beneficiary families. The scheme aims to reduce out-of-pocket expenditure for medical treatments and achieve Universal Health Coverage by offering cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals across the country. As of the latest data, over 23.39 crore beneficiaries have been verified and issued Ayushman Cards, with ₹61,501 crore worth of free treatment provided under the scheme till date.
objectives: Provide health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization
Reduce out-of-pocket expenditure for medical treatments
Ensure cashless access to healthcare services at empanelled hospitals
Promote gender equity in access to quality healthcare
Incentivize and encourage healthcare providers to focus on delivering patient-centric services
Strengthen fraud detection and claims adjudication through digital initiatives like AB PM-JAY Auto-Adjudication Hackathon
Expand hospital empanelment and improve quality of care through performance-based incentives
eligibility: Beneficiaries are identified based on deprivation and occupational criteria from the Socio Economic Caste Census (SECC) database. The scheme covers poor and vulnerable families as per SECC data, including those in rural areas living in households with only one room, no adult member aged 16-59, female-headed households with no adult male member, households with disabled members and no able-bodied adult, SC/ST households, and landless households deriving major income from manual casual labour. In urban areas, beneficiaries include those working in specified occupational categories such as ragpickers, beggars, domestic workers, street vendors, construction workers, etc. The scheme is implemented in 36 States/UTs, with West Bengal being the latest to join.
benefits: Cashless treatment for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization up to Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year at any empanelled hospital across India. Coverage includes 1,949 procedures under 27 specialties, including medical oncology, emergency care, orthopaedics, and urology. Over 141 medical procedures are exclusively earmarked for women. Beneficiaries receive co-branded PVC Ayushman Cards. The scheme also provides gender-equitable access, with approximately 49% of Ayushman Card recipients being women and over 48% of authorized hospital admissions availed by women. Additional benefits include portability of benefits across states, coverage for pre-existing diseases from day one, and no cap on family size or age.
financial_support: The scheme provides a health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year on a family floater basis, meaning the total amount can be used by one or all members of the family. There is no cap on the number of family members or age. The financial support is entirely funded by the Government of India, with funds allocated to States/UTs for implementation. As of the latest data, ₹61,501 crore worth of free treatment has been authorized under the scheme, and over 5 crore hospital admissions have been authorized. The National Health Authority (NHA) oversees the financial management, including disbursement of funds to States/UTs and monitoring of utilization.
application_process: 1. Check eligibility via the official NHA website or Ayushman App using mobile number or ration card details. 2. Visit a nearby Common Service Centre (CSC) or empanelled hospital with required documents. 3. Submit documents for verification. 4. Upon verification, the Ayushman Card is generated and issued. 5. Use the Ayushman Card for cashless treatment at any empanelled hospital by presenting it along with ID proof. 6. Hospitals submit claims to NHA through the transaction management system for reimbursement.
required_documents: Proof of identity (Aadhaar card, Voter ID, PAN card, etc.)
Proof of address (ration card, electricity bill, etc.)
Ration card or SECC verification document
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year.
contact_details: Toll-Free Call Centre No: 14555 (NHA/PM-JAY) | Email: webmaster-pmjay@nha.gov.in
caveats: Benefits are limited to secondary and tertiary care hospitalization only; outpatient care is not covered.
Treatment must be availed only at empanelled hospitals under the scheme.
The scheme does not cover expenses outside hospitalization such as OPD consultations, diagnostics, or medicines unless part of hospitalized treatment.
Beneficiaries must ensure their details are updated in the SECC database for continued eligibility.
Fraudulent claims may lead to penalties and debarment of hospitals or agencies.
The Ayushman Card must be renewed periodically as per state-specific guidelines.
